title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Compose Watch Inner Loop
summary: Developers need fast source sync and optional debugging while CI needs isolated dependencies, deterministic tests, and no production-only side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose watch inner loop increment in development and test Compose workflow matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.
Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.

version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example override bind-mounts production paths, watch rebuilds on generated files, and dev and test profiles silently change dependencies. Render every file combination and isolate disposable state. Competency probe: Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Watch Inner Loop, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to development and test Compose workflow matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-4
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose development testing 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Compose Watch replaces application-level hot reload and always works through bind mounts. This fails the Docker Compose Watch Inner Loop contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
